title: Sizzling National Fajita Day Fun
story: Sizzling skillets fill restaurants across America every August 18 as people celebrate National Fajita Day! This tasty holiday honors the popular Tex-Mex dish that started with grilled beef strips served on tortillas. Today, families and friends gather at Mexican restaurants to enjoy fajitas made with chicken, beef, shrimp, or vegetables. The best part is hearing that exciting sizzle when the hot cast-iron plate arrives at your table, loaded with peppers and onions. Many people love building their own perfect fajita by adding cheese, salsa, guacamole, and sour cream to warm tortillas.
activity_question: What makes fajitas exciting when they arrive at your table?
activity_choices: The sizzling sound from the hot plate
activity_answer: The sizzling sound from the hot plate
bonus_fact: The word 'fajita' comes from the Spanish word for 'little belt' or 'strip.'

title: Scoops Meet Crust: Ice Cream Pie Day!
story: Every August 18, dessert lovers across America celebrate National Ice Cream Pie Day by creating the ultimate frozen treat! This special day combines two beloved desserts into one amazing creation. People make ice cream pies by filling graham cracker crusts, chocolate cookie crusts, or pastry shells with their favorite ice cream flavors. Popular combinations include mint chocolate chip with chocolate crust, strawberry ice cream with vanilla wafer crust, or cookies and cream filling. Many families visit ice cream shops that offer special pie flavors just for this day. Some people get creative by adding whipped cream, hot fudge, or fresh berries on top. It's the perfect summer celebration!
activity_question: What do people typically use as the base for ice cream pies?
bonus_fact: Some ice cream pies are so tall they need to be cut with a warm knife!

title: Bad Poetry Day: Terrible Verses Welcome!
story: Every August 18, people celebrate Bad Poetry Day by writing the worst poems they can possibly create! This silly holiday encourages everyone to throw grammar out the window, make terrible rhymes, and use the most ridiculous words imaginable. Schools hold bad poetry contests where kids compete to write the most cringe-worthy verses. Social media fills with hilariously awful haikus and limericks that make people laugh instead of cry. The worse your poem sounds, the better you're doing! It's the one day when being bad at something is actually the goal, and everyone gets to be a gloriously terrible poet.
activity_question: What makes a poem perfect for Bad Poetry Day?
follow_up_question: What would you write a hilariously bad poem about?

title: Shopping From Your Couch Day
story: Every August 18, people celebrate National Mail Order Catalog Day by flipping through colorful catalogs and shopping online from home! This fun holiday honors the tradition of browsing through pages of cool stuff and ordering it to your door without ever leaving your pajamas.
activity_question: What do people mainly do to celebrate National Mail Order Catalog Day?
activity_choices: Shop from catalogs and online from home
Visit every store in their neighborhood
activity_answer: Shop from catalogs and online from home
bonus_fact: The first mail order catalog was created way back in 1872!

title: Custard's Last Stand: National Vanilla Custard Day
story: Spoons everywhere celebrate on August 18 for National Vanilla Custard Day! This creamy, dreamy dessert gets its own special day when bakeries showcase their fluffiest custard tarts, ice cream shops serve up frozen custard scoops, and home bakers whip up homemade batches. Unlike regular pudding, true custard contains eggs that make it extra rich and smooth. People share photos of their custard creations online and try new flavors, though vanilla remains the classic favorite that started it all.
activity_question: What ingredient makes custard different from regular pudding?
bonus_fact: Custard was accidentally invented when a cook's pudding recipe went wonderfully wrong!

title: Paws for a Cause Day
story: Every August 18, animal lovers across the country celebrate National Homeless Animals Day by helping pets who need families. People visit animal shelters to volunteer, donate blankets and toys, or even adopt a furry friend. Many families use this special day to spread awareness by sharing photos of adoptable pets on social media with hashtags like #AdoptDontShop. Some communities organize adoption events in parks where dozens of dogs and cats get to meet potential new families. It's amazing how one day can help so many animals find their forever homes!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Homeless Animals Day?
follow_up_question: What would you do to help homeless animals in your community?
